title: WildClawBench: A Benchmark for Real-World, Long-Horizon Agent Evaluation
core_claim: The paper introduces WildClawBench, a native-runtime benchmark of realistic long-horizon CLI agent tasks, and argues that current frontier models still struggle substantially when evaluated in actual tool-using environments rather than synthetic sandboxes.
likely_red_flags: Benchmark paper rather than a new model or algorithm, so research novelty may be limited to evaluation methodology
Performance claims depend on benchmark design choices, harness selection, and grader reliability
Strong results may be sensitive to task curation and the specific CLI runtimes used
Abstract reports broad frontier-model comparisons, but the benchmark may still be narrow in domain coverage despite 60 tasks
